title: advanced
body: {"category":"Advanced","templates":[{"name":"Automated Measurements Suite (Legacy 5k/7k/70k)","description":"Setup and read multiple measurements with statistics - for MSO/DPO 5k, 7k, 70k series","backend":"pyvisa","deviceType":"SCOPE","commandSet":"MSO_DPO_5k_7k_70K","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"connect","label":"Connect","params":{}},{"id":"2","type":"write","label":"Enable Statistics","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:STATIstics:MODe ALL"}},{"id":"g1","type":"group","label":"Configure Measurements","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"3","type":"write","label":"Meas1: Frequency","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS1:TYPe FREQuency"}},{"id":"4","type":"write","label":"Meas1 Source","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS1:SOUrce1 CH1"}},{"id":"5","type":"write","label":"Meas2: Amplitude","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS2:TYPe AMPlitude"}},{"id":"6","type":"write","label":"Meas2 Source","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS2:SOUrce1 CH1"}},{"id":"7","type":"write","label":"Meas3: Rise Time","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS3:TYPe RISe"}},{"id":"8","type":"write","label":"Meas3 Source","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS3:SOUrce1 CH1"}},{"id":"9","type":"write","label":"Meas4: Duty Cycle","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS4:TYPe PDUty"}},{"id":"10","type":"write","label":"Meas4 Source","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS4:SOUrce1 CH1"}}]},{"id":"11","type":"write","label":"Single Acquisition","params":{"command":"ACQuire:STOPAfter SEQUENCE"}},{"id":"12","type":"write","label":"Start","params":{"command":"ACQuire:STATE ON"}},{"id":"13","type":"sleep","label":"Wait for Complete","params":{"duration":1}},{"id":"g2","type":"group","label":"Read All Results","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"14","type":"query","label":"Read Frequency","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS1:VALue?","saveAs":"frequency"}},{"id":"15","type":"query","label":"Read Amplitude","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS2:VALue?","saveAs":"amplitude"}},{"id":"16","type":"query","label":"Read Rise Cycle","params":{"command":"MEASUrement:MEAS4:VALue?","saveAs":"duty"}}]},{"id":"18","type":"disconnect","label":"Disconnect","params":{}}]},{"name":"FFT Spectrum Analysis","description":"Capture FFT spectrum with custom window","backend":"pyvis

title: basic
body: {"category":"Basic","templates":[{"name":"Quick Steps UI Demo","description":"Complete reference showing every step type - connect, write, query, recall, save, sleep, python, and more","backend":"pyvisa","deviceType":"SCOPE","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"connect","label":"Connect to Scope","params":{"instrumentIds":[],"printIdn":true}},{"id":"g1","type":"group","label":"SCPI Commands","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"2","type":"write","label":"Write Command","params":{"command":"CH1:SCALE 1.0"}},{"id":"3","type":"query","label":"Query with Variable","params":{"command":"*IDN?","saveAs":"idn"}},{"id":"4","type":"set_and_query","label":"Set and Verify","params":{"command":"CH1:SCALE","cmdParams":[],"paramValues":{}}}]},{"id":"g2","type":"group","label":"Recall Types","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"5","type":"recall","label":"Factory Reset","params":{"recallType":"FACTORY","filePath":"","reference":"REF1"}},{"id":"6","type":"recall","label":"Recall Session (.tss)","params":{"recallType":"SESSION","filePath":"C:/TekScope/test.tss","reference":"REF1"}},{"id":"7","type":"recall","label":"Recall Settings (.set)","params":{"recallType":"SETUP","filePath":"C:/TekScope/config.set","reference":"REF1"}},{"id":"8","type":"recall","label":"Recall Waveform (.wfm)","params":{"recallType":"WAVEFORM","filePath":"C:/TekScope/golden.wfm","reference":"REF1"}}]},{"id":"g3","type":"group","label":"Save Operations","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"9","type":"save_screenshot","label":"Screenshot (Modern MSO5/6)","params":{"filename":"screen_modern.png","scopeType":"modern","method":"pc_transfer"}},{"id":"10","type":"save_screenshot","label":"Screenshot (Legacy 5k/7k/70k)","params":{"filename":"screen_legacy.png","scopeType":"legacy","method":"pc_transfer"}},{"id":"11","type":"save_waveform","label":"Save Waveform Data","params":{"source":"CH1","filename":"waveform.bin","format":"bin"}}]},{"id":"g4","type":"group","label":"Utility Steps","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"id":"12","type":"sleep","label":"Wait 500ms","params":{"duration":0.5}},{"id":"13","type":"error_check","label":"Check Errors","params":{"command":"ALLEV?"}},{"id":"14","type":"comment","label":"Comment (not executed)","params":{"text":"This is documentation only"}},{"id":"15","type":"python","label":"Custom Python","params":{"code":"print(f'IDN: {idn}')\nresul

title: screenshot
body: {"category":"tm_devices Examples","templates":[{"name":"Screenshot Capture","description":"Save screenshots using tm_devices high-level API","backend":"tm_devices","deviceType":"SCOPE","deviceDriver":"DPO70K","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"write","label":"Add MATH Channel","params":{"command":"scope.add_new_math(\"MATH1\", \"CH1\")"}},{"id":"2","type":"write","label":"Turn On CH2","params":{"command":"scope.turn_channel_on(\"CH2\")"}},{"id":"3","type":"write","label":"Set Horizontal Scale","params":{"command":"scope.set_and_check(\":HORIZONTAL:SCALE\", 100e-9)"}},{"id":"4","type":"write","label":"Save Screenshot (timestamped)","params":{"command":"scope.save_screenshot()"}},{"id":"5","type":"write","label":"Save Screenshot (named)","params":{"command":"scope.save_screenshot(\"example.png\")"}},{"id":"6","type":"write","label":"Save Screenshot (advanced)","params":{"command":"scope.save_screenshot(\"example.jpg\", colors=\"INVERTED\", local_folder=\"./images\", device_folder=\"./device_folder\", keep_device_file=True)"}}]}]}

title: tekexpress
body: {"category":"tekexpress","templates":[{"name":"PCIe Gen3 Compliance","description":"PCIe Gen3 Tx compliance test workflow","backend":"pyvisa","source":"tekexpress","steps":[{"type":"write","label":"Set Link Speed","params":{"command":"TEKEXP:VALUE GENERAL,\"Lane Count\",\"4\""}},{"type":"write","label":"Run Compliance Tests","params":{"command":"TEKEXP:STATE RUN"}},{"type":"query","label":"Check Status","params":{"command":"TEKEXP:STATE?","saveAs":"status"}}]}]}

title: tekhsi
body:
{"category":"TekHSI","templates":[{"name":"PyVISA + TekHSI Hybrid","description":"Command & control using PyVISA and waveform data via TekHSI","backend":"hybrid","source":"TekHSI/examples/hybrid_pyvisa.py","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"query","label":"Identify Instrument","params":{"command":"*IDN?","saveAs":"idn"}},{"id":"2","type":"write","label":"Hide Waveform","params":{"command":"DISPlay:WAVEform OFF"}},{"id":"3","type":"write","label":"Manual Timebase","params":{"command":"HOR:MODE MAN"}},{"id":"4","type":"write","label":"Record Length","params":{"command":"HOR:MODE:RECO 2500"}},{"id":"5","type":"python","label":"Stream Loop","params":{"code":"for i in range(10):
    with scope.access_data():
        waveform = scope.get_data('ch1')
        print(f"{waveform.source_name}_{i}:{waveform.record_length}")"}},{"id":"6","type":"write","label":"Show Waveform","params":{"command":"DISPlay:WAVEform ON"}},{"id":"7","type":"disconnect","label":"Disconnect","params":{}}]},{"name":"tm_devices + TekHSI Hybrid","description":"Command & control using tm_devices and waveform data via TekHSI","backend":"hybrid","source":"TekHSI/examples/hybrid_tm_devices.py","deviceType":"SCOPE","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"query","label":"Identify Instrument","params":{"command":"scope.commands.idn.query()","saveAs":"idn"}},{"id":"2","type":"write","label":"Hide Waveform","params":{"command":"scope.commands.display.waveform.write('OFF')"}},{"id":"3","type":"write","label":"Manual Timebase","params":{"command":"scope.commands.horizontal.mode.write('MAN')"}},{"id":"4","type":"write","label":"Record Length","params":{"command":"scope.commands.horizontal.mode.recordlength.write('2500')"}},{"id":"5","type":"python","label":"Stream Loop","params":{"code":"for i in range(10):
    with scope.access_data():
        waveform = scope.get_data('ch1')
        print(f"{waveform.source_name}_{i}:{waveform.record_length}")"}},{"id":"6","type":"write","label":"Show Waveform","params":{"command":"scope.commands.display.waveform.write('ON')"}},{"id":"7","type":"disconnect","label":"Disconnect","params":{}}]},{"name":"TekHSI Standalone with Plotting","description":"Pure TekHSI waveform capture and plotting with matplotlib","backend":"tekhsi","source":"TekHSI/examples/plotting.py","steps":[{"id":"1","type":"connect","label":"Connect via TekHSI gRPC","params":{}},{"id":"2","type":"python","label":"Impo

title: Template JSON Structure — Complete Schema
body: Every TekAutomate template is a JSON object inside a category wrapper. Top-level: { "category": "Name", "templates": [ ... ] }. Each template object MUST have: name (string), description (string), steps (array). Recommended: backend ('pyvisa'|'tm_devices'|'tekhsi'|'hybrid'), deviceType ('SCOPE'|'AWG'|'SMU'|etc.), deviceDriver (e.g. 'MSO6B'). Steps array contains step objects with id, type, label, params. Multi-device steps add boundDeviceId. Group steps add children array and optional collapsed boolean.

title: Required Pattern — Connect First, Disconnect Last
body: CRITICAL RULE: Every template MUST start with a 'connect' step and end with a 'disconnect' step. The connect step establishes instrument connections; use empty instrumentIds [] to connect all enabled devices. The disconnect step closes all connections. No template should omit these. For single-device templates, connect params can be empty {}. For multi-device, use instrumentIds: [] (empty = all) or specific aliases. Steps between connect and disconnect form the workflow. This is the most important structural rule for template validity.

title: Step Type Reference — All Valid Step Types
body: Valid step types: connect (params: instrumentIds, printIdn — always first), disconnect (params: instrumentIds — always last), write (params: command — send SCPI, no response), query (params: command, saveAs — REQUIRED to store result), set_and_query (params: command, cmdParams, paramValues — set then verify), sleep (params: duration in seconds, decimals OK e.g. 0.5), recall (params: recallType FACTORY|SETUP|SESSION|WAVEFORM, filePath, reference), save_screenshot (params: filename, scopeType modern|legacy, method), save_waveform (params: source, filename, format bin|wfm|csv, width, encoding, start, stop), error_check (params: command usually ALLEV?), comment (params: text — not executed), python (params: code — custom Python), group (children array, collapsed boolean — container), tm_device_command (params: code, model, description — tm_devices backend only), sweep (params: variableName, start, stop, step, saveResults, resultVariable — iterate values).

title: Golden Example: Complete Step Types (Flat List)
body: Golden reference showing every valid step type in a flat sequential list. Demonstrates connect, write, query (with saveAs), set_and_query, sleep, all recall types (FACTORY, SESSION, SETUP, WAVEFORM), save_screenshot (modern and legacy), save_waveform, error_check, comment, python, tm_device_command, and disconnect. This is the canonical reference for step parameters.

title: Golden Example: All Step Types with Groups
body: Golden reference showing every step type organized into logical groups. Groups: SCPI Commands (write, query, set_and_query), Recall Types (FACTORY, SESSION, SETUP, WAVEFORM), Save Operations (screenshot modern/legacy, save_waveform), Utility Steps (sleep, error_check, comment, python). Each group has collapsed: false and children array. This demonstrates best practices for organizing complex templates.

title: Golden Example: Perfect Single Device Template
body: Best-practice single-device template showing the recommended workflow: connect with printIdn, reset (*RST), wait for reset, channel configuration group, stop/start acquisition, wait for trigger, stop acquisition, *OPC? wait, save waveform, error check, disconnect. This is the canonical example for single-device scope automation. Note: all commands go to the first enabled device by default (no boundDeviceId needed).

title: Screenshot Template: Modern MSO5/6 (Correct 3-Step)
body: The CORRECT screenshot template for MSO5/6/MSO5B/MSO6B series scopes. Uses only 3 steps: connect, python block, disconnect. The python block contains the complete screenshot workflow: create local directory, create remote temp directory (C:/Temp), SAVE:IMAGE:COMPOSITION NORMAL, SAVE:IMAGE with remote path, time.sleep(1.0) to wait (NOT *OPC? which causes UnicodeDecodeError), set timeout to 30000, FILESYSTEM:READFILE, read_raw(), restore timeout, write bytes locally, FILESYSTEM:DELETE cleanup. CRITICAL: Do NOT use query('*OPC?') after FILESYSTEM:READFILE — use time.sleep(1.0) instead. Modern scopes do NOT support HARDCOPY commands.

title: Screenshot Template: Legacy 5k/7k/70k (HARDCOPY Method)
body: The CORRECT screenshot template for MSO/DPO 5k/7k/70k series scopes. Uses 3 steps: connect, python block, disconnect. The python block: create local dir, create remote dirs (C:/TekScope/Temp), HARDCOPY:PORT FILE, HARDCOPY:FORMAT PNG (was previously broken — literal '${format}'), HARDCOPY:FILENAME with remote path, HARDCOPY START, time.sleep(1.0) to wait (NOT *OPC?), set timeout to 30000, FILESYSTEM:READFILE, read_raw(), restore timeout, save locally, FILESYSTEM:DELETE cleanup. CRITICAL: Legacy scopes do NOT support SAVE:IMAGE — must use HARDCOPY. The HARDCOPY:FORMAT bug (literal ${format}) was fixed in pythonGenerators.ts line 1264.

title: Screenshot Template: MSO/DPO 70000 Series (EXPort Method)
body: Screenshot for MSO/DPO 70000 series (MSO73304DX, MSO70404C, DPO70404C etc). Uses EXPort commands, NOT SAVE:IMAGE or HARDCOPY. Flow: EXPort:FILEName set path, EXPort:FORMat PNG, EXPort:VIEW FULLSCREEN, EXPort:PALEtte COLOR, EXPort START (case-sensitive!), *OPC? wait, FILESYSTEM:READFILE transfer, FILESYSTEM:DELETE cleanup. Default working directory is C:/TekScope. Tested on MSO73304DX FW 10.14.1.

title: Scope Family Screenshot Command Summary
body: MSO 4/5/6 Series (MSO44/54/58/64/68B): Trigger=SAVE:IMAGe "path.png", format from extension, view=SAVE:IMAGe:VIEWTYpe FULLScreen, colors=SAVE:IMAGe:COMPosition NORMal, default path=C:/Temp. NOT SUPPORTED: SAVE:IMAGe:FILEFormat, SAVE:IMAGe:INKSaver, SAVE:IMAGe:LAYout, EXPort, HARDCopy. MSO/DPO 70000 Series (MSO73304DX etc): Trigger=EXPort START (case-sensitive!), EXPort:FILEName for path, EXPort:FORMat PNG, EXPort:VIEW FULLSCREEN, EXPort:PALEtte COLOR, default path=C:/TekScope. NOT SUPPORTED: SAVE:IMAGe, HARDCopy:DATA?. Both: FILESystem:READFile for transfer (raw binary, no IEEE header), FILESystem:DELEte for cleanup.

title: Recall Template: Factory Reset
body: Factory reset restores scope to default settings. No filePath needed. recallType: 'FACTORY'. This is equivalent to sending *RST but through the recall mechanism.

title: Recall Template: Settings (.set file)
body: Recall a settings-only file (.set). Restores instrument configuration (channel scales, trigger, horizontal, etc.) but NOT waveform data. recallType: 'SETUP'. filePath must point to a .set file on the scope filesystem (e.g. C:/TekScope/config.set).

title: Recall Template: Session (.tss file)
body: Recall a full session file (.tss). Restores EVERYTHING: settings, waveforms, measurements, math, search, reference waveforms. This is the most complete recall type. recallType: 'SESSION'. filePath must point to a .tss file. Add sleep(2.0) after recall to allow full load.

title: Recall Template: Waveform (.wfm to REF)
body: Recall a waveform data file (.wfm) into a reference channel (REF1-REF4). recallType: 'WAVEFORM'. filePath points to .wfm file. reference specifies which REF channel (REF1, REF2, etc.). After recall, reference waveform is available for comparison.

title: Save Template: Waveform Data
body: Save waveform data from a channel. source: CH1-CH4. filename: output file. format: 'bin' (fastest, binary), 'wfm' (Tek format), 'csv' (human readable). width: 1 or 2 bytes. encoding: 'RIBinary' (signed) or 'RPBinary' (unsigned). start/stop: data point range (null = all). For binary: configure DATa:SOUrce, DATa:ENCdg, DATa:WIDth, then CURVe? to fetch.

title: Save Template: Screenshot (Step Type)
body: The save_screenshot step type captures a screen image. scopeType: 'modern' (MSO5/6, uses SAVE:IMAGE) or 'legacy' (5k/7k/70k, uses HARDCOPY). method: 'pc_transfer' (save on scope then transfer). For reliable operation, use a python block instead of save_screenshot step — the python block gives full control over timeouts and error handling.

title: Multi-Device Template Pattern with boundDeviceId
body: Multi-device templates control multiple instruments. Key rules: 1) Connect all devices first (instrumentIds: []), 2) Use boundDeviceId on every step to specify target device, 3) Device aliases (e.g. 'scope-uuid', 'psu-uuid') match user-configured device entries, 4) Without boundDeviceId, commands go to first enabled device, 5) Disconnect all at end. Groups can also have boundDeviceId which applies to all children. Device selection priority: step boundDeviceId > parent group boundDeviceId > default (first device).

title: TekExpress Template Pattern — SOCKET Connection, State Polling
body:
TekExpress templates use PyVISA SOCKET backend on port 5000. CRITICAL RULES: 1) Connection: TCPIP::host::5000::SOCKET with write_termination='
' and read_termination='
', 2) Commands use TEKEXP:* namespace, 3) NO *OPC? support — use TEKEXP:STATE? polling loop instead, 4) Must handle popups with TEKEXP:POPUP? check during WAIT/ERROR states, 5) Never generate raw socket.sendall() code — only .write()/.query(), 6) Termination handled by PyVISA config — don't add 
 in commands. State machine: RUN → RUNNING → WAIT/ERROR (handle popup) → COMPLETE.

title: Template with Groups: Setup → Configure → Acquire → Measure → Save
body: Demonstrates group-based organization for complex workflows. Five groups: Setup Phase (*RST, *CLS, wait), Channel Configuration (enable, scale, position), Acquisition (stop after sequence, start, wait for trigger, *OPC?), Measurements (add pk2pk, set source, read value), Save Results (screenshot, waveform). Groups help users understand workflow phases and can be collapsed for readability.

title: Measurement Sweep: Iterate Scales and Capture
body: Demonstrates iterating through different scale settings (1V, 500mV, 100mV per division), capturing frequency measurements at each scale, and taking screenshots. Uses groups to organize each scale point. Adds frequency measurement first, then loops through scales. Each group: set scale, settle (sleep 0.5), read measurement, screenshot. Final python block prints all results. Note: this uses manual group repetition, not the sweep step type.

title: Session Recall and Validation Template
body: Load a session file (.tss) and validate that settings were applied correctly. Pattern: connect, recall SESSION, sleep(2.0) for load, query group to check CH1:SCALE?, HORizontal:SCALE?, TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH1?, then python block with assert statements to validate. Useful for test setup verification.

title: tm_devices High-Level API Template
body: Template using the tm_devices backend with high-level Python API calls instead of raw SCPI. Commands use scope.commands.* or scope.turn_channel_on() etc. Backend MUST be set to 'tm_devices'. deviceDriver specifies exact model (e.g. MSO6B). Commands are Python method calls, not SCPI strings.

title: Sweep Step Type: Voltage Sweep with SMU
body: The sweep step type iterates over a range of values. params: variableName (e.g. 'voltage'), start, stop, step, saveResults (boolean), resultVariable. Children steps use ${variableName} in commands for substitution. Useful for characterization sweeps. Note: sweep is different from group — it automatically iterates and can collect results.

title: Template Validation Rules — What Makes a Template Valid
body: VALID template requirements: 1) Has name and description, 2) Has non-empty steps array, 3) First step is type='connect', 4) Last step is type='disconnect', 5) Specifies backend (recommended), 6) Query steps have saveAs, 7) Multi-device steps have boundDeviceId, 8) Commands use full SCPI syntax (not abbreviations), 9) Labels are descriptive. INVALID patterns (common mistakes): Missing connect/disconnect, multi-device without boundDeviceId, tm_devices commands without backend:'tm_devices', query without saveAs, using *OPC? after FILESYSTEM:READFILE, using HARDCOPY on modern scopes or SAVE:IMAGE on legacy scopes, connect all but disconnect specific (inconsistent), sweep type used where group is needed.

title: File Type Reference — .SET, .TSS, .WFM Extensions
body: .SET = Settings only file. Contains: channel scales, trigger configuration, horizontal settings, display settings. Does NOT contain: waveform data, measurements, reference waveforms. Use recallType: 'SETUP' to load. .TSS = Full session file. Contains: ALL settings + waveform data + measurements + math channels + search + reference waveforms. Most complete recall type. Use recallType: 'SESSION'. Takes longer to load (add sleep 2.0). .WFM = Waveform data file. Contains: raw waveform sample data for one channel. Used with recallType: 'WAVEFORM' and reference: 'REF1'-'REF4' to load into reference channels. For saving: save_waveform step produces .bin, .wfm, or .csv depending on format param.

title: Backend Specification in Templates
body: Always specify backend when template uses backend-specific features. PyVISA ('pyvisa'): Standard SCPI commands like 'CH1:SCALE 1.0'. tm_devices ('tm_devices'): High-level API like 'scope.commands.ch[1].scale.write(1.0)'. TekHSI ('tekhsi'): gRPC API like 'scope.ch[1].scale = 1.0'. Hybrid ('hybrid'): Mix SCPI (via PyVISA) for config and TekHSI for fast waveforms. IMPORTANT: tm_devices commands in a pyvisa template will fail. Backend determines how commands are interpreted and what code is generated.

title: Common Template Patterns
body: Pattern 1 — Setup → Execute → Save: connect, *RST, sleep(1.0), configure channels, start acquisition, wait, stop, save data, disconnect. Pattern 2 — Group Organization: connect, group(Setup), group(Configure), group(Measure), group(Save), disconnect. Pattern 3 — Query and Process: connect, query to get value (saveAs), python block to process/validate, disconnect. Pattern 4 — Recall and Verify: connect, recall session/setup, sleep for load, query settings, python assert, disconnect. Pattern 5 — Multi-Step Screenshot: connect, python block with complete screenshot code, disconnect. Pattern 6 — Sweep: connect, setup instruments, sweep step with children, cleanup, disconnect.

title: XML Example: Basic Scope Setup and Waveform Capture
body: Blockly XML for basic oscilloscope setup and waveform capture. Blocks: connect_scope, set_device_context, enable_channel (CH1), configure_channel (SCALE 1.0, OFFSET 0.0, COUPLING DC, TERMINATION ONEMEG), scpi_write (horizontal scale), acquisition_reset, single_acquisition, wait_for_opc, save_waveform (CSV), disconnect. Validated and fixed — configure_channel now includes TERMINATION field. Generates clean Python code.

title: XML Example: Voltage Sweep with SMU (Multi-Device Blockly)
body: Blockly XML for multi-device voltage sweep. Uses tm_devices backend. Blocks: connect_scope x2 (scope + SMU), set_device_context (switch between devices), python_code (SMU output on, set voltage), controls_for loop (v = 1 to 5), acquisition_reset, single_acquisition, wait_for_opc, measurement_immediate (PK2PK), disconnect x2. Demonstrates device context switching and for loops in Blockly. Validated, no changes needed.

title: XML Example: TekExpress Compliance Test Blockly Workflow
body: Blockly XML for TekExpress compliance testing (validated for USB, PCIe, DisplayPort). Blocks: connect_tekexpress, tekexp_write (multiple SCPI), tekexp_query, tekexp_select_device, tekexp_select_test, tekexp_set_value (DUTID), tekexp_run, tekexp_wait_state (COMPLETE with popup handling), tekexp_export_report, python_code, wait_seconds (uses <field> not <value> — was a bug fixed in TekExpress_USB.xml), disconnect. Key fix: wait_seconds uses FieldNumber (<field name="SECONDS">2</field>), NOT <value> with shadow blocks.

title: XML/Blockly Validation Rules and Common Errors
body: XML validation rules: 1) All block types must be registered (connect_scope, scpi_write, tekexp_write, etc.), 2) Field names must match block definitions exactly, 3) wait_seconds uses <field name="SECONDS">value</field> NOT <value> with shadow blocks (FieldNumber not value input), 4) configure_channel must include TERMINATION field, 5) All <next> tags must be properly closed, 6) Variable declarations must match usage, 7) Mutation tags present where needed. Common fix: TekExpress_USB.xml had 6 wait_seconds blocks using wrong format (value→field). Common fix: basic_setup_waveform.xml missing TERMINATION field.

title: Device Binding Rules — boundDeviceId Usage
body: Device binding determines which instrument receives each command. Rules: 1) boundDeviceId uses device ALIAS (not UUID), set by user in device configuration, 2) If boundDeviceId not specified, command goes to default (first enabled) device, 3) For single-device templates, binding is unnecessary, 4) Device selection priority: step boundDeviceId > parent group boundDeviceId > default device, 5) Connect and disconnect can use instrumentIds array for specific devices or empty [] for all, 6) Each device can have different backend (scope=tm_devices, smu=pyvisa), 7) Commands within groups inherit group's device unless overridden.

title: Template Creation Checklist
body: Before creating: Determine single vs multi-device, choose backend, identify device type(s), plan connection strategy. Structure checks: Has name and description, has non-empty steps array, starts with connect, ends with disconnect, specifies backend. Connection checks: Connect is first, disconnect is last, handles single/multi-device correctly, printIdn for verification. Multi-device checks: Uses boundDeviceId for device-specific commands, device aliases documented, all devices connected at start and disconnected at end. Command quality: Full SCPI syntax (not abbreviations), library commands when possible, query steps have saveAs, labels are descriptive. Code generation: Generates valid Python, no syntax errors, device references correct, backend-specific commands correct.

title: Common Template Pitfalls to Avoid
body: Pitfall 1: Missing connect/disconnect — every template needs both. Pitfall 2: Multi-device without boundDeviceId — commands go to wrong device. Pitfall 3: Missing backend specification — tm_devices commands in pyvisa template fail. Pitfall 4: Inconsistent device handling — connect all but disconnect only one. Pitfall 5: Using *OPC? after FILESYSTEM:READFILE — causes UnicodeDecodeError from PNG binary data in buffer. Use time.sleep(1.0) instead. Pitfall 6: Using HARDCOPY on modern scopes (MSO5/6) — not supported, use SAVE:IMAGE. Pitfall 7: Using SAVE:IMAGE on legacy scopes (5k/7k/70k) — not supported, use HARDCOPY. Pitfall 8: HARDCOPY:FORMAT ${format} literal — was a generator bug, now fixed. Pitfall 9: Query without saveAs — result is lost.

title: Save Operations — Setup, Session, Waveform, Image
body: Save SETUP: SAVE:SETUP "filepath.set" — saves instrument settings only. Save SESSION: SAVe:SESsion "filepath.tss" — saves full session with waveforms. Save WAVEFORM: Use save_waveform step type with source (CH1), filename, format (bin/wfm/csv). Internally configures DATa:SOUrce, DATa:ENCdg, DATa:WIDth, then CURVe? for data. Save IMAGE: Use save_screenshot step type OR python block. Modern: SAVE:IMAGE "path.png". Legacy: HARDCOPY:PORT FILE + HARDCOPY:FORMAT PNG + HARDCOPY:FILENAME + HARDCOPY START. Both transfer via FILESYSTEM:READFILE.

title: Scope Type Detection for Template Selection
body: Detect scope series from *IDN? response to choose correct commands. MSO 4/5/6 series (model starts with MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, or contains TEKSCOPESW): Use SAVE:IMAGe, default path C:/Temp. MSO/DPO 70000 series (model starts with MSO7 or DPO7): Use EXPort commands, default path C:/TekScope. Legacy 5k/7k (DPO5000, DPO7000, MSO5000): Use HARDCOPY commands, default path C:/TekScope/Temp. Python detection: parse parts[1] from IDN string, check prefix (MSO7/DPO7 → mso70k, MSO4/5/6 → mso456).
